commit.message:
feat: self-improving skill for all agents + perplexity web search with MCP Playwright fixes (#7)

Adds the `self-improving` skill to all five OpenClaw agents and enables
Perplexity-based web search for research-capable agents. Fixes the
`perplexity-webui-search` skill which used non-standard Playwright MCP
APIs throughout.

## Self-improving skill — all agents

Added `self-improving` to `skills` in every `openclaw.json` (ceo, pm,
tech-lead, biz-research, cost-controller).

Each agent's `AGENTS.md` updated with:
- Tiered memory guidance (`memory/` for factual continuity,
`~/self-improving/` for execution quality)
- Weekly heartbeat check to promote corrections and keep `memory.md` ≤
100 lines

## Perplexity web search — research agents

Added `perplexity-webui-search` to ceo, tech-lead, and biz-research
(`openclaw.json` + `AGENTS.md`).

## Perplexity skill — MCP Playwright compliance fixes

The skill was written against a non-existent selector-based API. Fixed
to use the standard snapshot-first `ref` pattern:

| Before (non-standard) | After (MCP standard) |
|---|---|
| `browser_navigate({ url, waitUntil })` | `browser_navigate({ url })` |
| `browser_type({ selector, text })` | `browser_snapshot()` →
`browser_type({ element, ref, text })` |
| `browser_press({ selector, key })` | `browser_press_key({ key })` |
| `browser_evaluate({ script })` | `browser_evaluate({ function })` |
| `browser_get_text({ selector })` | `browser_evaluate({ function })` |
| `browser_choose_file({ selector, files })` | `browser_file_upload({
paths })` |

Added a **MCP Playwright Tool Reference** section explaining the
snapshot-first workflow with a concrete example showing how to read
`ref` values from the accessibility tree. Replaced all LaTeX formatting
with standard Markdown.

## Perplexity skill — direct Playwright MCP setup (v1.2.0)

Replaced the ClawHub super-skill dependency
(`Spiceman161/playwright-mcp` via `@lobehub/market-cli`) with the
official **`@playwright/mcp`** package used directly:

- `requires.skills` dependency removed; replaced with `requires.bins:
[npx]`
- Prerequisites updated to `npm install -g @playwright/mcp@latest` with
an MCP server config JSON snippet
- Troubleshooting updated to use `npx playwright install chromium`

commit.message:
Phase 6.2: Implement PM4 command buffer packets for AMD GPUs

Implement PM4 (Packet Manager version 4) packet encoding for AMD RDNA2/3 GPUs.
PM4 is AMD's command submission format, equivalent to Intel's 3DSTATE commands.

Implementation:
- Created render-sys/src/pm4.rs with comprehensive PM4 packet builder (587 LOC)
- PM4Builder for Type 3 packet encoding and accumulation
- Core packets: NOP, SET_CONTEXT_REG, SET_SH_REG, SET_UCONFIG_REG
- Draw commands: DRAW_INDEX_AUTO with primitive topology support
- Synchronization: EVENT_WRITE, SURFACE_SYNC, ACQUIRE_MEM, RELEASE_MEM
- Pipeline control: CLEAR_STATE, SET_BASE
- Coherency control flags for cache management (coher_cntl, gcr_cntl modules)
- PrimitiveType enum for topology (PointList, LineList, TriangleList, etc.)

Testing:
- 14 comprehensive PM4 packet tests (100% pass rate)
- Total: 215 Rust tests passing
- All Go tests passing (including race detector)
- Static linking verified

Compliance:
- Zero regressions in complexity/duplication metrics
- Follows project patterns (similar to Intel CommandBuilder)
- Reference: Mesa RADV, AMD register databases

Phase 6.2 complete. Ready for Phase 6.3 (AMD ISA Backend).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 6.1: Implement AMD AMDGPU kernel IOCTL wrappers

Implements comprehensive AMD GPU driver support infrastructure:

Core Implementation:
- Created render-sys/src/amd.rs (~660 LOC) with full IOCTL interface
- 8 core ioctls: GEM_CREATE, GEM_MMAP, GEM_WAIT_IDLE, GEM_VA,
  CTX, CS, WAIT_CS, INFO
- GemCreate: Buffer allocation with domain/placement control
- GemVa: Explicit GPU VA mapping (map/unmap operations)
- ContextOp: GPU context lifecycle (alloc/free/query)
- CommandSubmission: IB submission infrastructure for GFX/COMPUTE
- WaitCommandSubmission: Fence-based synchronization
- DeviceInfo: GPU properties query (family, CUs, clocks, etc.)

GPU Detection:
- Extended GpuGeneration enum with AMD variants:
  * AmdRdna1 (Navi 10/12/14, RX 5000)
  * AmdRdna2 (Navi 21-24, RX 6000, Steam Deck)
  * AmdRdna3 (Navi 31-33, RX 7000, Phoenix)
- detect_amd_generation() via AMDGPU_INFO_DEV_INFO
- Family ID mapping: 143 (RDNA1), 144/146 (RDNA2), 148/149 (RDNA3)
- Added is_amd()/is_intel() helper methods

Buffer Allocation:
- Extended DriverType enum with Amdgpu variant
- Implemented allocate_amdgpu() in BufferAllocator
- VRAM+GTT domain placement with CPU access support
- Automatic tiling (hardware-managed, no explicit mode)

Testing & Validation:
- 8 new AMD ioctl unit tests (GemCreate, GemVa, ContextOp, etc.)
- 3 new GpuGeneration tests (is_amd, is_intel, generation names)
- All 209 Rust tests passing (201 passed, 8 GPU tests ignored)
- All Go tests passing (30/30 packages)
- Zero regressions in Go code complexity/duplication metrics

Architecture Notes:
- Mirrors Intel i915/Xe IOCTL pattern for consistency
- Uses nix crate request_code_readwrite! macro for ioctl numbers
- Follows Mesa RADV and Linux kernel amdgpu conventions
- DMA-BUF export compatible (via existing prime_handle_to_fd)
- Explicit VM management (AMDGPU_GEM_VA for address mapping)

Phase Status:
- Phase 6.1 (AMDGPU Kernel IOCTLs): ✅ Complete
- Phase 6.2 (PM4 Command Buffers): Next
- Phase 6.3 (RDNA ISA Backend): Deferred

Files Modified:
- render-sys/src/amd.rs: New file, 672 lines

commit.message:
feat: add agent parameter to session creation for pre-selecting custom agents (#722)

Add an optional `agent` field to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig across
all four SDKs (Node.js, Python, Go, .NET) that allows specifying which custom
agent should be active when the session starts.

Previously, users had to create a session and then make a separate
`session.rpc.agent.select()` call to activate a specific custom agent. This
change allows setting the agent directly in the session config, equivalent to
passing `--agent <name>` in the Copilot CLI.

The `agent` value must match the `name` of one of the agents defined in
`customAgents`.

Changes:
- Node.js: Added `agent?: string` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.ts for both session.create and session.resume RPC calls
- Python: Added `agent: str` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.py for both create and resume payloads
- Go: Added `Agent string` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.go for both request types
- .NET: Added `Agent` property to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  updated copy constructors, CreateSessionRequest/ResumeSessionRequest records,
  and CreateSessionAsync/ResumeSessionAsync call sites
- Docs: Added "Selecting an Agent at Session Creation" section with examples
  in all 4 languages to custom-agents.md, updated session-persistence.md and
  getting-started.md
- Tests: Added unit tests verifying agent parameter is forwarded in both
  session.create and session.resume RPC calls

Closes #317, closes #410, closes #547

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix OpenClaw review findings for network, doctor, and worker setup

* fix(build,network): openclaw detach fix, loopfs timeout, docker coexistence

build.py — systemctl proxy script:
- Set HOME=/home/node to prevent EACCES chdir when SSH inherits HOME=/root
- Add </dev/null so openclaw does not inherit the SSH channel stdin
- Add & disown so non-interactive bash (su -c) exits immediately without
  waiting for the backgrounded openclaw process — fixes systemctl start
  openclaw SSH timeout in the reconciler

build.py — loopfs extract:
- Scale extract timeout: max(300, rootfs_size_mb // 8) seconds
  Tar-extracting node_modules onto a loop ext4 is inode-bound and can
  easily exceed 30 s on pd-standard disks for 4 GB+ images

setup-worker-node.sh — section 6:
- Add Docker coexistence check/apply for DOCKER-USER iptables rules
  so SmolVM tap+ traffic is not blocked by Docker's FORWARD policy

* fix(build): scale umount timeout same as extract

After extracting 4 GB+ of node_modules onto a loop-mounted ext4,
the kernel must flush a large dirty-page cache to disk before umount
can complete. On pd-standard this exceeds the 30 s default timeout.
Pass the same extract_timeout (max(300, rootfs_size_mb // 8)) to the
umount call so both operations use a size-scaled limit.

- Use awk to safely strip /etc/fstab swap entries
- Provide vendor-accurate KVM nx_huge_pages modprobe hints
- Enforce strict fail on missing DOCKER-USER chain

The grep -oP flag relies on PCRE which is unsupported on macOS and minimal

type redefinition in the REPL for all users, even those not using
browser hot-reload — an unacceptable regression.

Opt-in: SAGEFS_HOT_RELOAD=1 enables DevReload browser auto-refresh and
method detouring, with the documented tradeoff that type redefinition
is disabled in that session.
